exemplar.title: Determining the Surface Area of a Chicken Egg Using Derivatives and Integration

detailedExemplarData.title: Determining the Surface Area of a Chicken Egg Using Derivatives and Integration

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequential development guiding the reader through story, data, modelling and conclusion
Well-organized with coherent section headings, embedded figures and tables
Mostly concise and relevant content supporting the aim
weaknesses: Extended Mean Value Theorem exposition interrupts flow
Minor formatting slips (misaligned equations, repeated captions)
Could be more concise by removing repeated images and theoretical digressions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is coherent and logically developed (Band 3), with a clear introduction, body and conclusion, effective use of headings and embedded visuals. However, occasional digressions into theory and minor formatting issues prevent it from reaching the highest band. Overall it meets the criteria for a score of 3.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate mathematical notation and wellâdefined variables
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, schematics)
Good logical flow from data to modelling and numerical evaluation
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in notation (missing brackets, mixed text styles)
Occasional algebraic slips and dense theoretical paragraphs
A missing parenthesis in the integral reduces clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is mostly strong, with correct and largely consistent notation, effective representations and clear logical structure (Band 3). Minor notational inconsistencies and occasional dense exposition limit it from the highest band, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: Personal narrative and choice of object show significant independent thinking
Custom dataâgathering strategy (four orientations, use of Desmos/Excel) demonstrates a clear personal approach
Creative adaptation of surfaceâofârevolution techniques to an egg model
weaknesses: Derivations follow standard references, limiting originality
Approach is solid but not highly innovative or exceptional
Could further extend or vary methodology for deeper personal engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration exhibits significant independent thinking and a clear personal voice (Band 2), but the methods remain largely conventional and do not reach the highly innovative level required for Band 3.

strengths: Thoughtful error analysis with literature comparison and percentage errors
Discussion of practical implications and future improvements
Reflection on modelling assumptions and measurement uncertainty
weaknesses: Depth of reflection is moderate rather than substantial
Limited quantification of errors and uncertainty
Future suggestions are mentioned but not explored in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present throughout with meaningful analysis of results and process (Band 2). While critical and linked to applications, it lacks the deeper, comprehensive evaluation and insight required for the top band, meriting a score of 2.

strengths: Comprehensive use of relevant SL mathematics (regression, differentiation, integration)
Mathematics is fully at or slightly beyond course level
Correct application of powerârule, integral setup, and percentageâerror checks
weaknesses: Minor algebraic and rounding slips in numerical work
Opaque calculator output not fully justified
Derivation steps (frustum formula) could be more rigorously linked to MVT
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant, wellâapplied at SL level and shows thorough understanding (Band 5). Minor accuracy and exposition issues prevent a Band 6, so a score of 5 accurately reflects the competence and occasional errors.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments follow a logical chain from data collection to modelling, derivation and numerical evaluation. Explanations are generally clear, but occasional algebraic slips and dense paragraphs on theory reduce overall clarity slightly, placing the work in the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ band.

Derivatives are computed correctly and integral set-up is sound; the final numeric result is plausible. Minor algebraic/rounding slips and the opaque calculator output (6414914865 before scaling) show understanding is good but not entirely secure, so accuracy falls short of the top two bands.

The exploration includes tables of coordinates, multiple regression graphs, photographs of measurement technique and schematic diagrams of frustums. These representations complement each other and aid understanding, demonstrating effective â though not exemplary â use of multiple forms.

The student guides the reader from the personal story through data collection, modelling, derivation, calculation, error analysis and conclusion in a clear sequential order. Paragraphs link logically and section headings are used effectively. Minor digressions (lengthy recount of Mean Value Theorem theory) occasionally interrupt the flow, preventing the work from reaching the consistently excellent standard required for the top band.

Creativity is seen in adapting surface-of-revolution ideas to an egg and in combining two validation methods, yet the mathematical techniques employed are conventional. Initiative is good but not âexceptionalâ across the entire work.

The error analysis compares results with two literature-based estimates and calculates percentage error, demonstrating thoughtful â though not deeply probing â critical analysis of accuracy and validity.

Outcomes are evaluated via percentage error and discussion of practical implications (coating, packaging). Proposed future improvements are mentioned but not explored in depth, indicating a clear but not comprehensive evaluation.

The student reflects on modelling assumptions (smooth shell, symmetry) and measurement uncertainty, and links findings to potential industrial applications. Reflection is present throughout, but depth is moderate rather than substantial.

Appropriate symbols (â«, Ï, â, dy/dx) and function notation are used throughout and variables are defined. Minor inconsistencies (missing brackets in the long integral, occasional use of plain text in place of italics) mean usage is âmostly consistentâ rather than flawless.

The student designs and executes a personal strategy (photographing, scaling, using Excel/Desmos, averaging four views) that is clearly their own, giving a clear personal voice. The approach is solid but not highly innovative, aligning with the middle band.

Most material directly supports the aim; however, extended theoretical exposition of Rolleâs/Mean Value Theorem and repeated images inflate the length to 21 pages. With tighter editing the same argument could be delivered more concisely, so the submission meets âmostly relevantâ rather than âall content relevantâ.

Calculus of surface area of revolution, polynomial regression, numerical integration and percentage-error checks are all directly relevant to determining egg surface area, representing effective and fairly comprehensive use of mathematics.

Choosing a personally meaningful object, gathering primary data, fitting several regressions and extending the investigation to four orientations show significant independent thinking beyond textbook examples. Nevertheless, the derivations closely follow standard calculus references, so the evidence is substantial but not outstanding.

A recognizable introduction, body and conclusion are present, and subsections are numbered coherently. Figures, tables and formulae are embedded close to the relevant discussion. Page numbers and captions support navigation. A few formatting slips (mis-aligned equations, repeated scatterplot captions) detract slightly from otherwise strong organisation.

The mathematics (degree-6 regression, derivative, definite integral over 0â5.5 cm, calculator evaluation) is squarely at SL level and occasionally ventures slightly beyond typical syllabus expectations, showing full appropriateness for the course.

annotations.comment: The assumption f(x_z)âf(x_{z-1}) requires an error estimate for Îxâ0 to justify neglecting variation; consider adding a bound from Taylorâs theorem.
There is a missing parenthesis in the integral expression; ensure the integrand reads â(1+(fââ²(x))Â²) dx for full mathematical clarity.
Equation numbering is inconsistent: referencing 'Equation 1' for both the regression and f(x) definition can confuse the reader; renumber uniquely.
The powerârule derivative is applied correctly, showing solid understanding of differentiation at SL level.
The numerical integral result is reported without intermediate validation steps; include a small Riemannâsum check or screenshots of your TIâ84 process for transparency.
Reporting the final result as 64.2 cmÂ² is clear, but ensure that rounding reflects the least precise measurement to maintain correct significant figures.
The studentâs vivid personal narrative effectively demonstrates clear independent thinking and a personal connection to the problem, enriching the exploration.
The aim is well articulated and linked to realâworld applications in industry, showing purposeful use of relevant mathematics.
Measurement procedure is described clearly, but the student should state the measurement uncertainty (e.g. Â±0.1 cm) to acknowledge random error.
When introducing the surfaceâofârevolution concept, include a citation or reference for the derivation source to strengthen academic rigour.
Consider increasing point density near regions of high curvature or using nonâuniform spacing to capture the eggâs shape more accurately.
Table 2.1.1 is informative but would benefit from clearer gridlines and a concise caption placement for improved readability.
The regression equation is clearly presented, but adding explicit parentheses around each coefficient and power term would eliminate potential ambiguity.
The interpretation of the RÂ² value is well integrated, demonstrating effective use of statistical representation.
Assumptions are clearly laid out, but the student could strengthen analysis by discussing how asymmetry might quantitatively affect the result.
The extended Mean Value Theorem exposition interrupts the flow; summarise key points concisely and reference full derivation in an appendix.
The progression to a limit formulation for arc length is communicated clearly, showing strong logical development of calculus concepts.
Figure 3.2.2 caption repeats the section title; refine it to highlight how the diagram illustrates the hypotenuse interpretation.
When simplifying â((Îx)Â²+(Îy)Â²), clarify the substitution Îy = f'(x*)(Îx) by referencing the specific MVT result used.
The conceptual link between rotating the curve and summing frustum areas is described clearly, reinforcing logical structure.
When introducing the frustum formula, explicitly show how r=(râ+râ)/2 follows from the MVT step to strengthen the derivation.
Exploring four orientations shows creative initiative and enhances the robustness of the model.
Averaging results across angles using a mean is correctly applied, reflecting appropriate mathematical usage.
The literature comparison is thorough and supports the validity of results, demonstrating thoughtful critical analysis.
The percentâerror calculation is sound, but discuss whether systematic or random errors dominate to deepen critical analysis.
Using an independent arccosâbased formula for crossâvalidation shows deep reflection and critical analysis of methods.
Limitations section is insightful; consider quantifying how shell roughness might add a measurable surface area percentage.
The conclusion restates findings clearly, but could be strengthened by reflecting on lessons learned and proposing concrete next steps.
